quote: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
author: Lao Tzu

arifos_mapping.physics: Action requires structured energy and direction; entropy decreases only with work input.
arifos_mapping.math: Consistency requires invariant maintenance; small steps accumulate to large displacements.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Language must encode constraint, not just possibility; the first step is a speech act of commitment.

quote: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

arifos_mapping.physics: The observer is part of the observed system; measurement disturbs the field.
arifos_mapping.math: Confidence intervals must include uncertainty; overfitting is the illusion of knowledge.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Words of certainty often mask ignorance; epistemic humility is a precondition of truth.

quote: The ink of the scholar is more sacred than the blood of the martyr.
author: Prophet Muhammad

arifos_mapping.physics: Information persists longer than kinetic energy; entropy of knowledge is lower than entropy of war.
arifos_mapping.math: Knowledge accumulation dominates over zero-sum conflict; the scholar's mark is a constructive proof.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: The pen outweighs the sword in lasting value; text outlives trauma.

quote: What I cannot create, I do not understand.
author: Richard Feynman

arifos_mapping.physics: Understanding is the ability to reconstruct a system from first principles.
arifos_mapping.math: Constructive proof is stronger than existential proof; if you can build it, you understand it.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Explanation without construction is hearsay; the builder's testimony is admissible evidence.

quote: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Viktor Frankl

arifos_mapping.physics: Purpose provides an attractor state for resilient dynamics; systems with attractors recover from perturbation.
arifos_mapping.math: Robustness is higher when the objective function is clear; drift without purpose increases variance.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Meaning is the load-bearing structure of endurance; narrative coherence sustains action.

quote: Premature optimization is the root of all evil.
author: Donald Knuth

arifos_mapping.physics: Optimizing local minima can destabilize global structure; premature forcing creates brittle systems.
arifos_mapping.math: Big-O analysis should follow correctness proof; optimize only after profiling identifies bottlenecks.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Measure before you claim; the premature declaration of efficiency is a category error.

quote: If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.
author: African proverb

arifos_mapping.physics: Distributed systems have higher mean time to failure but slower initial velocity; redundancy costs latency.
arifos_mapping.math: Coordination cost is O(n^2) but robustness scales with redundancy; the union outlasts the singleton.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Speed is solitary; distance is communal; the first-person singular is a sprint, the plural is a marathon.

quote: Those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it.
author: George Santayana

arifos_mapping.physics: Systems with no memory are Markovian and repeat errors; hysteresis is a feature, not a bug.
arifos_mapping.math: Time-series without autoregressive terms have high variance; the past is a predictor of the future.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Memory is the defense against cyclical failure; to forget is to reset the error counter.

quote: Di mana bumi dipijak, di situ langit dijunjung.
author: Malay proverb

arifos_mapping.physics: Local boundary conditions determine system behavior; no universal solution fits all substrates.
arifos_mapping.math: Context-sensitive rules outperform universal heuristics; the optimal policy is state-dependent.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Adat tempat bergantung; respect the local grammar before speaking.

quote: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

arifos_mapping.physics: Systems without feedback decay; the unobserved system drifts to maximum entropy.
arifos_mapping.math: Self-consistency requires recursive evaluation; a system that cannot inspect itself is incomplete.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Life without examination is mere existence; the interrogative is the highest mood.

quote: Sebab nila setitik, rosak susu sebelanga.
author: Malay proverb

arifos_mapping.physics: Small perturbations can cascade to system failure; the butterfly effect is real in social systems.
arifos_mapping.math: Sensitivity to initial conditions; one bad datum can corrupt the entire training set.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: A single drop of ink spoils the milk; one lie poisons the well of trust.

quote: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.
author: Albert Einstein

arifos_mapping.physics: Intelligence is the capacity to update internal models from external signals; plasticity is survival.
arifos_mapping.math: Bayesian updating rate correlates with predictive accuracy; the rigid prior is a cognitive prison.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Intelligence is measured by the speed of belief revision; the wise change their minds.

quote: In God we trust; all others bring data.
author: W. Edwards Deming

arifos_mapping.physics: Reality is the final arbiter; belief must align with measurement or it is fiction.
arifos_mapping.math: Hypothesis testing requires data; priors alone are insufficient for inference.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Trust is earned by evidence, not granted by title; the credential is not the proof.

quote: He who knows himself knows his Lord.
author: Ibn Arabi
theme: self-knowledge as gateway to deeper truth

arifos_mapping.physics: The observer is entangled with the observed; you cannot measure the system without measuring yourself.
arifos_mapping.math: Self-reference is necessary for consistent epistemology; Gödel's lens applies to the self.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Knowing the self is the foundation of all knowing; the first person is the root of grammar.

quote: The child who is not embraced by the village will burn it down to feel its warmth.
author: African proverb

arifos_mapping.physics: Systems without heat dissipation experience thermal runaway; exclusion is a heat source.
arifos_mapping.math: Excluded agents have incentive to defect in game-theoretic frames; the Nash equilibrium becomes destructive.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Exclusion is the seed of destruction; the unheard voice becomes the shout of fire.

quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

arifos_mapping.physics: Repeated force shapes material; character is the plastic deformation of practice.
arifos_mapping.math: Expected value is the sum over time; transient excellence has low integral.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Identity is the cumulative function of repeated action; you are your habits.

quote: 己所不欲，勿施於人。
author: Confucius

arifos_mapping.physics: Feedback loops return effects to their origin; action at a distance is local in the long run.
arifos_mapping.math: Symmetric payoff matrices favor cooperation; the golden rule is a Nash equilibrium.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Do not impose on others what you yourself do not desire; the imperative is reciprocal.

quote: Nothing is more precious than independence and freedom.
author: Hồ Chí Minh

arifos_mapping.physics: Autonomy is the capacity to maintain internal state against external forcing; it is self-regulation.
arifos_mapping.math: Independence is the condition where boundary conditions do not dominate the solution space.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: Freedom is the non-negotiable baseline of dignity; it is the axiom, not the theorem.

quote: As a person acts, so they become.
author: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad

arifos_mapping.physics: Systems evolve according to their dynamics; you are your trajectory, not your initial condition.
arifos_mapping.math: Identity is the integral of action over time; the derivative of character is choice.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: You become what you repeatedly do; the verb precedes the noun.

quote: The greatest enemy of knowledge is not ignorance, it is the illusion of knowledge.
author: Stephen Hawking
theme: false expertise is more dangerous than admitted ignorance

arifos_mapping.physics: False models persist when feedback is suppressed; the illusion of knowledge is a closed system.
arifos_mapping.math: Overconfidence inflates variance; humility reduces mean squared error.
arifos_mapping.linguistic: The illusion of knowing prevents actual learning; certainty is the enemy of curiosity.
